notes.anchor: Sp. Mummius was among the ten legates
notes.note: Sp. Mummius, brother of the consul L. Mummius and himself present at Corinth in 146 BC; Cicero corrects his own guess about which Mummius sat on the board of ten commissioners.
L. Mummius Achaicus, consul 146 BC and sacker of Corinth; Cicero decides it was he, not Spurius, who served among the ten legates, "for he was the one at Corinth."

The "Torquatus" is the roll or book of Cicero's De Finibus named after its Epicurean speaker; Cicero is sending Atticus a copy. He routinely refers to the dialogue's books by their chief interlocutor.
Silius owned a property Cicero was considering for the Tullia shrine; Cicero presses Atticus to negotiate and to pin down the date of an upcoming transaction or auction.
